ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Na ayɛyɛdeɛ no sene dii n’anim; na ɔno deɛ, ɔtenaa nsraban no mu anadwo no. Yakob ne ɔbɔfoɔ bi tentam
translated: And the present went on before him; but he himself lodged that night in the camp. Jacob and a certain angel wrestled

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: meresiane makɔhwɛ sɛ wɔayɛ sɛdeɛ nteamu a ɛduru me nkyɛn no teɛ no a; na sɛ ɛnte saa, mahunu.
translated: I want to go and check if what I have been told is true; and if it is not, I will find out.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: Na ɔmaa n’ani so hwɛe, na hwɛ, mmarima baasa abɛgyina ne ntentensoɔ. Na ɔhunuu wɔn no, ɔfirii ne ntomadan pono ano tuu mmirika kɔhyiaa wɔn, na ɔbɔɔ ne mu ase
translated: Then he lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing by him. And when he saw them, he ran from the tent door to meet them and bowed himself to the ground.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Onyankopɔn mma wo ɔsoro bosuo ne asase mu sradeɛ bi, ne aburoo ne nsa pii.
translated: May God grant you heavenly dew and earthly dew, and plenty of corn and wine.

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Na abɔfra no nyinii, na wɔtwaa no nufoɔ. Na da a wɔtwaa Isak nufoɔ no, Abraham too pono kɛseɛ. Wɔpamo Hagar ne Ismael
translated: And the child grew, and he was weaned. And on the day that Isaac was weaned, Abraham held a great feast. They drove out Hagar and Ishmael.
